title: Building Enterprise AI Agents in Regulated Industries
gist: BCG argues that regulated enterprises in sectors like health care and banking cannot scale agentic AI through fragmented pilots or after-the-fact controls; instead they need a common platform that embeds orchestration, guardrails, evaluations, governance, and human oversight from the start. It presents a three-layer enterprise architecture and a reuse-configure-build decision framework for CIOs and CDAOs.

insights.claim: For regulated businesses scaling agentic AI, a common platform of shared standards and consistent controls is no longer optional but is the operating foundation for AI-first businesses.
insights.speaker: Daniel Martines
insights.so_what: Enterprises without a unified agentic platform will scale slower and at higher cost; platform vendors and integrators serving regulated sectors are positioned to capture this demand.
insights.quote: For agentic AI at scale, the common platform is no longer optional. It is the operating foundation for AI-first businesses.
insights.locator: A common platform provides that foundation of capability.

insights.claim: Without a common platform, enterprise AI efforts become fragmented, harder to govern, more expensive to scale, and slower to deliver impact.
insights.speaker: Daniel Martines
insights.so_what: Fragmentation is the core risk to watch when assessing an enterprise's AI maturity and cost trajectory.
insights.quote: Without a common platform, AI efforts become fragmented, harder to govern, more expensive to scale, and slower to deliver impact.
insights.locator: Enterprise value from agentic AI starts with a platform

insights.claim: A common agentic platform must standardize critical layers including orchestration, model access, evaluations, guardrails, memory and knowledge management, security, and monitoring.
insights.speaker: Daniel Martines
insights.so_what: This layer list is a checklist for evaluating enterprise AI platform completeness and vendor coverage.
insights.quote: It standardizes the critical layers required to scale safely: orchestration, model access, evaluations, guardrails, memory and knowledge management, security, and monitoring.
insights.locator: A common platform provides that foundation of capability.

insights.claim: Because agentic systems can observe, plan, act, and respond with increasing independence, the absence of defined controls can create material regulatory and operational risk.
insights.speaker: Daniel Martines
insights.so_what: Autonomy without controls is the specific failure mode that regulators and risk officers will scrutinize; controls maturity is a due-diligence signal.
insights.quote: Without defined controls, that autonomy can create material regulatory and operational risk.
insights.locator: This matters because agentic systems can observe, plan, act

insights.claim: In regulated industries, enterprises need clear rules on what agents can access, what actions they can perform, and how those actions remain traceable and auditable, especially where agents perform regulated activities that create legal exposure.
insights.speaker: Daniel Martines
insights.so_what: Traceability and auditability of agent actions become mandatory compliance requirements in health care and banking deployments.
insights.quote: Enterprises need clear rules and standards regarding what agents can access, what actions they can perform, and how those actions will remain traceable and auditable.
insights.locator: The needs are even more acute in regulated industries

insights.claim: The controls required for regulated agentic AI cannot be easily implemented by separate business units or added after agents are already operating at scale, so CIOs should address them early rather than after systems are embedded in workflows.
insights.speaker: Daniel Martines
insights.so_what: Retrofitting governance is a red flag; early-stage governance design predicts which enterprises will scale successfully.
insights.quote: The necessary controls cannot be easily implemented by separate business units or added after agents are already operating at scale.
insights.locator: The needs are even more acute in regulated industries

insights.claim: An end-to-end enterprise-grade agentic solution rests on three key layers: a semantic intelligence layer, a data abstraction layer, and a governance and operations layer.
insights.speaker: Daniel Martines
insights.so_what: This reference architecture is a template for assessing whether an enterprise AI stack covers coordination, data readiness, and governance.
insights.quote: An end-to-end, enterprise-grade solution accomplishes this by means of three key layers
insights.locator: To scale safely, agents should draw on a set of common components

insights.claim: A data abstraction layer unifies and normalizes data across systems of record to make enterprise information AI-ready within regulated governance boundaries.
insights.speaker: Daniel Martines
insights.so_what: Data unification within governance boundaries is a prerequisite investment before agentic use cases can deliver value.
insights.quote: A data abstraction layer unifies and normalizes data across systems of record, making enterprise information AI-ready within regulated governance boundaries.
insights.locator: A data abstraction layer unifies and normalizes data

insights.claim: Guardrails, evaluations, and common standards must be embedded from the start rather than added after agents are already in production.
insights.speaker: Daniel Martines
insights.so_what: Design-time governance is the differentiator between enterprise-scale AI and disconnected local experiments.
insights.quote: guardrails, evaluations, and common standards and rules are embedded from the start, not added after agents are already in production.
insights.locator: Together, these layers help ensure that agents operate within defined boundaries

insights.claim: Companies using common-platform approaches have driven a 25% productivity gain across the software development life cycle and a 20% to 30% improvement in software quality.
insights.speaker: Daniel Martines
insights.so_what: These benchmarks give investors concrete productivity and quality upside figures to test against vendor and enterprise ROI claims.
insights.quote: Companies have used these approaches to drive a 25% productivity gain across the software development life cycle and a 20% to 30% improvement in software quality.
insights.locator: Leaders are freed to focus on higher-value innovation

insights.claim: One large automotive client deployed a trusted GenAI-enabled knowledge assistant to achieve 50% to 75% faster search and more user-friendly access to enterprise knowledge assets.
insights.speaker: Daniel Martines
insights.so_what: Knowledge-search acceleration is an early, measurable agentic use case with quantified gains applicable across knowledge-heavy enterprises.
insights.quote: one large automotive client deployed a trusted GenAI-enabled knowledge assistant to achieve 50% to 75% faster search
insights.locator: Data readiness and security are important.

insights.claim: When deploying agents, CIOs should be selective by asking whether an existing agent can meet the need, whether the use case is a commodity capability, and whether the workflow requires custom logic or differentiated business outcomes.
insights.speaker: Daniel Martines
insights.so_what: This three-question filter governs where enterprises spend on custom builds versus reuse, shaping the addressable market for off-the-shelf agents.
insights.quote: In many cases, extending, reusing, or reconfiguring a proven agent is faster, cheaper, and easier to govern than building from scratch.
insights.locator: When deploying agents, CIOs should be selective

insights.claim: Most enterprises will require a hybrid model that combines reuse, configuration, and building while maintaining consistent governance across each.
insights.speaker: Daniel Martines
insights.so_what: The hybrid default means demand spans both reusable-agent platforms and custom development, not one or the other.
insights.quote: most enterprises will require a hybrid model that combines all three options—reuse, configuration, and building—while maintaining consistent governance across each.
insights.locator: In practice, most enterprises will require a hybrid model

insights.claim: Companies must decide where standardization is necessary and where federation makes more sense, managing some capabilities centrally while keeping others closer to specific domains or functions.
insights.speaker: Daniel Martines
insights.so_what: The centralize-versus-federate choice determines operating-model design and where control planes sit in the enterprise.
insights.quote: Companies also need to decide where standardization is necessary and where federation makes more sense.
insights.locator: Organizations need to make several foundational choices

insights.claim: In regulated industries human judgment and decision making remain invaluable, and human oversight should stay embedded within high-risk workflows and regulated activities.
insights.speaker: Daniel Martines
insights.so_what: Human-in-the-loop is a persistent requirement in regulated sectors, capping the degree of full automation and shaping labor and compliance costs.
insights.quote: Human oversight should remain embedded within high-risk workflows and regulated activities.

insights.claim: Agentic AI should not scale as a technology exercise but against clear value pools, productivity improvements, customer outcomes, and risk controls.
insights.speaker: Daniel Martines
insights.so_what: Tie platform investments to measurable outcomes; deployments unconnected to value pools signal weak ROI discipline.
insights.quote: It should scale against clear value pools, productivity improvements, customer outcomes, and risk controls.
insights.locator: Connect platform choices to measurable business outcomes.

insights.claim: A shared control plane helps maintain consistency, governance, and auditability as agentic AI adoption grows, and early platform and governance decisions shape how effectively organizations can scale.
insights.speaker: Daniel Martines
insights.so_what: Presence of a shared control plane is an indicator of scalable, auditable agentic AI maturity in an enterprise.
insights.quote: A shared control plane helps maintain consistency, governance, and auditability as adoption grows.
insights.locator: CIO and CDAO leaders should approach agentic AI with enterprise scale
